title: diospyros ferrea var. pubescens
intro: Diospyros ferrea var. pubescens, also known as the hairy persimmon, is a variety of Diospyros ferrea, a species of evergreen tree native to Australia. The hairy persimmon is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. The hairy persimmon can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used topically in the form of a poultice or cream to treat skin conditions and wounds.
